mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered months despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized collapse in household demand that depresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show positive median 60-day returns in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, positive industrial production growth outpaces rate hikes, confirming resilient manufacturing output that supports short-term equity price appreciation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of tightening periods with rising industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity that increases corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% coincide with positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates confirms structural labor market weakness that reduces aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields a positive mean return over 20-day horizons in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, accelerating retail sales growth signals rapid consumer confidence restoration that fuels immediate short-term equity market rallies.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during recovery phases with rising retail sales are negative in > 55% of observations.
